channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Conservative opposition wins German election and the far right is 2nd with strongest postwar result
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany's conservative opposition leader Friedrich Merz won a lackluster victory in a national election Sunday, while Alternative for Germany doubled its support in the strongest showing for a far-right party since World War II, projections showed.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sudan’s military breaks paramilitary group's siege of crucial city
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Sudan’s military on Sunday broke a more than yearlong siege on the crucial city of Obeid, restoring access to a strategic area in the south-central region and strengthening crucial supply routes in its nearly two years of war against a notorious paramilitary group, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Masses of mourners attend the funeral of Hezbollah leader Nasrallah, 5 months after his killing
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of people packed into a stadium in Beirut and nearby streets on Sunday for the funeral of Hezbollah's former leader, Hassan Nasrallah, nearly five months after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, ABBY SEWELL and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: They crossed the Darien Gap to reach the US. Now, boat-by-boat, migrants are returning
item.description: They once braved the jungles of the Darien Gap, trekking days along the perilous migrant passage dividing Colombia and Panama with a simple goal: seek asylum in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Matias Delacroix And Juan Zamorano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: American Airlines flight from New York to New Delhi lands safely in Rome after security concern
item.description: An American Airlines flight from New York to New Delhi was diverted to Rome due to a security concern the airline later said was not credible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zelenskyy says progress made on reaching an agreement with the US on rare minerals deal
item.description: Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says a contentious U.S. proposal that would have seen $500 billion worth of profits from Ukrainian rare minerals given to the United States as compensation for its wartime assistance to Kyiv has been taken off the table.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel sends tanks into West Bank for first time in decades, says fleeing Palestinians can't return
item.description: Israeli tanks have moved into the occupied West Bank for the first time in decades in what Palestinian authorities call a “dangerous escalation.”.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after Taliban lift suspension
item.description: Radio Begum launched on International Women’s Day in March 2021, five months before the Taliban seized power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Wounded, recovered and back to war. Ukrainian soldiers are returning to battle after amputation
item.description: DONETSK REGION, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian intelligence soldier doesn’t know how long his clinical death lasted after an explosive detonated beneath him. All Andrii Rubliuk remembers is overwhelming cold, darkness and fear.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, EVGENIY MALOLETKA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump-Putin summit preparations are underway, Russia says
item.description: Preparations are underway for a face-to-face meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin, Russia's deputy foreign minister said Saturday. It marked a clear departure from Western efforts to isolate Moscow over its war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered an asthmatic respiratory crisis that required oxygen, the Associated Press reported. The 88-year-old pope has been hospitalized for over a week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrian suspect in Berlin Holocaust Memorial stabbing wanted to kill Jews, investigators say
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — The suspect in a stabbing attack at Berlin's Holocaust Memorial that seriously injured a Spanish tourist is a Syrian refugee who apparently wanted to kill Jews, investigators said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran tests Trump as regime general says it will wipe Israel off the map
item.description: A general from Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps lashed out at Israel and the U.S. during military exercises, setting the stage for a new attack on the Jewish state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains of Shiri Bibas positively identified after eventually being handed over by Hamas
item.description: The body of a woman who was presumed to have been one of four dead hostages Hamas handed over to Israel was positively identified Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect arrested after stabbing at Berlin's Holocaust memorial near US embassy
item.description: German police arrested a suspect in a stabbing attack at Berlin's Holocaust memorial on Friday. No motive has been given for the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An encroaching desert threatens to swallow Mauritania’s homes and history
item.description: CHINGUETTI, Mauritania (AP) — For centuries, poets, scholars and theologians have flocked to Chinguetti, a trans-Saharan trading post home to more than a dozen libraries containing thousands of manuscripts. But it now stands on the brink of oblivion.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis is 'fine,' condition not life-threatening, doctors say
item.description: Pope Francis "is fine" as his medical condition is not life-threatening, but he is not being released from the hospital yet, this doctors in Italy announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sinn Féin leaders won't attend St. Patrick's event at White House to protest US stance on Gaza
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sinn Féin's leaders said Friday they will not attend a traditional St. Patrick's Day event next month at the White House as a protest against President Donald Trump's stance on Gaza.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 20 Mexican cities, 5 in US among 50 most dangerous in the world
item.description: At the top of the list is Haiti's Port-au-Prince with 139 homicides per 100,000 residents, followed by five cities in Mexico: Colima, Manzanillo, Acapulco, Tijuana and Ciudad Obregón.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu calls for 'revenge' after Hamas returns wrong remains
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is vowing revenge after Hamas delivered the body of an anonymous woman instead of Shiri Bibas in violation of the current ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European troops, US support are part of an emerging plan for Ukraine. But it faces many hurdles
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As the U.S. and Russia push ahead — so far without Ukraine at the table – on talks to end the war, political and military leaders in Europe are fleshing out details of a plan for European forces to help ensure Moscow doesn't attack again.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shocking deep sea discovery made in area where Bible says Moses parted Red Sea
item.description: Scientists have discovered an area in the Red Sea with natural death traps in the region believed to be the location where Moses parted the waters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel identifies remains of child hostages but says another body from Hamas was not their mother
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — The Israeli military said Friday it had positively identified the remains of two young hostages but another body released by Hamas under a ceasefire deal was not the boys’ mother as the militant group had promised.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, JOSEF FEDERMAN AND WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel's UN ambassador slams Hamas' 'evil and depraved' display of hostages' coffins
item.description: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk condemned Hamas' parading of the coffins containing Shiri, Ariel and Kfir Bibas and Oded Lifshitz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump warns Zelenskyy to quickly negotiate war's end with Russia or risk not having a nation to lead
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy that he “better move fast” to negotiate an end to Russia's invasion of Ukraine or risk not having a nation to lead.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, MIKE PESOLI and STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's envoy arrives in Kyiv as US policy shifts toward Russia
item.description: Keith Kellogg, the U.S. special envoy for Ukraine and Russia, arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday for talks with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and military commanders, as the U.S. shifted its policy away from years of efforts to isolate Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DeepSeek accused of sharing user data with TikTok owner ByteDance
item.description: "We confirmed DeepSeek communicating with ByteDance," a South Korean data protection regulator told Yonhap News Agency.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Earth’s solid metal core may be soft on the outside: Study
item.description: Rather than being perfectly ball-shaped, the Earth's inner core may actually resemble peaks and valleys.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation
